data.title: Frontage Holdings Sets 2026 AGM to Seek Mandates on Share Buybacks and Issuance
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Frontage Holdings Corp. ( (HK:1521) ) has issued an announcement.

Frontage Holdings Corporation has called its annual general meeting for 2 June 2026 in Shanghai, where shareholders will review and adopt the audited consolidated financial statements and directors’ and auditor’s reports for the year ended 31 December 2025. The agenda also includes the re-election of executive and independent non-executive directors, confirmation of directors’ remuneration, and the re-appointment of BDO Limited as auditor.

Shareholders will further be asked to approve a share repurchase mandate allowing the board to buy back up to 10% of the company’s issued shares, as well as a general mandate to issue additional shares and grant related options and awards within prescribed limits. These authorities, if granted, would give the board greater flexibility in capital management and financing, potentially affecting capital structure, shareholder returns and the company’s ability to respond to market opportunities over the coming year.

More about Frontage Holdings Corp.

Frontage Holdings Corporation is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong that operates in the life sciences and pharmaceutical-related services sector. The company provides research, development and related scientific services to biopharmaceutical clients, positioning itself within the broader contract research and development industry focused on drug discovery and development support.

Average Trading Volume: 119,764

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$2.02B

An update from Yankuang Energy Group Company Limited Class H ( (HK:1171) ) is now available.

Yankuang Energy Group Company Limited has proposed a new slate of directors for its tenth board session, including six directors and four independent non-executive director candidates, with their eligibility and independence for certain roles subject to review by the Shanghai Stock Exchange. The candidates, along with an employee representative director elected separately by staff, will be put to shareholders for approval at the 2025 annual general meeting, with three-year terms starting from the conclusion of that meeting.

The company also announced that several current directors, including one independent non-executive director who has reached the six-year regulatory limit, will step down at the end of the ninth board session, prompting a refresh of key board and committee positions. The outgoing directors have indicated they have no disagreements with the board or issues requiring shareholder attention, and the company has expressed its appreciation for their service as it prepares for the next governance cycle.

The company is governed by a board structure that includes executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors, as well as employee representative directors elected by staff.

An announcement from Hisense Home Appliances Group Co ( (HK:0921) ) is now available.

Hisense Home Appliances Group Co. has completed the regulatory filings for changes to its business registration following shareholder approval to amend its Articles of Association. The revised scope of business formally covers a wide array of activities, including internet sales, smart-home and IoT device manufacturing, software development, data processing, and the sale of certain medical devices and services. China Glass Holdings Limited has convened its annual general meeting for 25 June 2026 as an online virtual event, where shareholders will vote on the adoption of the audited financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025 and the related directors’ and auditor’s reports. data.title: Shandong Xinhua Pharma Q1 Profit Drops Despite Stable Revenue and Asset Base
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Shandong Xinhua Pharmaceutical Company Limited Class H ( (HK:0719) ).

Shandong Xinhua Pharmaceutical Company Limited reported essentially flat operating income of RMB 2.42 billion for the first quarter of 2026, while net profit attributable to shareholders fell 25.2% year on year to RMB 83.9 million and earnings per share dropped to RMB 0.12. The profit decline reflects higher operating and R&D costs and weaker operating cash flow, with net cash from operating activities swinging to an outflow of RMB 116.6 million, even as total assets were broadly stable and net assets attributable to shareholders edged up 2.7%, signaling margin pressure despite a largely unchanged revenue base.

The company’s weighted average return on net assets decreased to 1.59% from 2.21%, underscoring reduced profitability and efficiency in capital use. Management has emphasized that the figures are unaudited but prepared under Chinese accounting standards and cautioned shareholders and potential investors to exercise care when trading the company’s securities in light of the softer earnings and cash flow profile.

More about Shandong Xinhua Pharmaceutical Company Limited Class H

Shandong Xinhua Pharmaceutical Company Limited is a China-based pharmaceutical manufacturer listed in Hong Kong, operating through a group structure that consolidates its various subsidiaries. The company focuses on producing and selling pharmaceutical products and related healthcare offerings in both domestic and international markets.

data.title: Shenzhen Expressway Lifts Q1 Profit on Strong Core Earnings Despite Flat Revenue
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Shenzhen Expressway Co ( (HK:0548) ) has issued an update.

Shenzhen Expressway Corporation Limited, a PRC-based toll road and expressway operator listed in Hong Kong and Shanghai, earns most of its income from operating, investing in and managing highway infrastructure. The group’s business is embedded in China’s transport infrastructure sector, where regulated tariffs and traffic volumes drive earnings and cash flows and shape its long-term investment capacity.

In the first quarter of 2026, the company posted revenue of RMB 1.76 billion, virtually flat year-on-year, while total profit rose 8.3% and net profit attributable to shareholders increased 6.5%. Excluding non-recurring items, core net profit grew 10.7% and operating cash flow climbed 4.8%, indicating stronger underlying profitability despite modest top-line movement.

Basic and diluted earnings per share edged down to RMB 0.192 from RMB 0.203 and weighted average return on equity slipped to 2.10%, reflecting capital base expansion that outpaced profit growth. Total assets were broadly stable at about RMB 71.0 billion, while equity attributable to shareholders rose 1.6%, suggesting a slightly stronger balance sheet even as short-term returns on equity moderated.

The unaudited figures signal resilient operations and improving earnings quality for Shenzhen Expressway, which may reassure investors about cash generation in a capital-intensive sector. However, the combination of flat revenue and lower ROE underscores the ongoing challenge of extracting higher returns from a growing asset base in a heavily regulated toll road market.

More about Shenzhen Expressway Co

Shenzhen Expressway Corporation Limited is a Chinese joint stock company that operates, invests in and manages highways and related infrastructure projects. Listed in Hong Kong and Shanghai, the group focuses on toll road and expressway operations, generating revenue primarily from traffic flows across its portfolio of transport assets in the People’s Republic of China.

The company’s activities position it within the broader transport infrastructure and toll road sector, where cash flow stability, capital intensity and regulatory oversight are key factors for shareholders and creditors. Its performance is closely tied to regional economic activity, traffic volumes and policy changes affecting road tariffs and concessions.

As an infrastructure operator, Shenzhen Expressway balances operational efficiency with long-term asset management, and its quarterly disclosures provide investors with updates on revenue trends, profitability and balance sheet strength. These metrics help stakeholders assess the sustainability of dividends, funding capacity for new projects and the company’s competitive standing in China’s expressway market.

Average Trading Volume: 2,309,803

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

Current Market Cap: HK$24.1B

An announcement from Guangdong Kanghua Healthcare Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:3689) ) is now available.

Guangdong Kanghua Healthcare Group has announced a change to the book closure period for its register of members related to the 2025 annual general meeting. The new closure dates run from 15 June 2026 to 18 June 2026, during which no share transfers will be processed, and shareholders on the register as of 18 June 2026 will be eligible to attend and vote.

data.title: Guangdong Land Holdings Narrows Loss as High-Margin Projects Drive 107% Revenue Surge
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Guangdong Land Holdings Limited ( (HK:0124) ) has shared an update.

Guangdong Land Holdings Limited reported a sharp rebound in its first-quarter 2026 performance, with revenue surging 107.5% year-on-year to about HK$1.58 billion, driven mainly by higher sales of gross floor area from properties held for sale. The group’s loss attributable to shareholders narrowed dramatically to roughly HK$3 million from HK$154 million a year earlier, helped by stronger margins at the Guangzhou GDH Future City project, while equity attributable to owners rose modestly on the back of renminbi appreciation, suggesting a stabilising financial position despite a still-challenging property market.

Total assets edged down 1.2% to HK$33.15 billion as at 31 March 2026, but net asset value per share increased 2.2% to HK$1.82, underscoring improved balance sheet quality. The shift towards delivering higher-margin projects such as Guangzhou GDH Future City, alongside contributions from Foshan One Mansion and Zhongshan GDH City, indicates a strategic focus on more profitable developments that could support future earnings recovery and offer some reassurance to investors and other stakeholders monitoring the group’s operational turnaround.

More about Guangdong Land Holdings Limited

Guangdong Land Holdings Limited is a Hong Kong-listed property developer incorporated in Bermuda, focusing on the development and sale of residential and mixed-use projects in mainland China. The group’s portfolio includes large-scale projects such as Foshan One Mansion, Guangzhou GDH Future City and Zhongshan GDH City, positioning it in the mainland urban real estate market.

data.title: Legend Holdings Sets 2025 AGM to Reshape Governance and Expand Financing Mandates
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Legend Holdings Corporation Class H ( (HK:3396) ).

Legend Holdings Corporation has called its 2025 annual general meeting for June 26, 2026, in Beijing, where shareholders will review the company’s 2025 directors’ and supervisors’ reports, audited financial statements, profit distribution plan, and the re-appointment of PricewaterhouseCoopers as independent auditor. The meeting will also vote on appointing Yu Hao as an executive director, abolishing the board of supervisors with related amendments to the articles and governance rules, and granting broad mandates to the board to issue up to RMB35 billion in debt, issue shares including treasury shares, and repurchase H shares, moves that collectively expand the company’s financing flexibility and reshape its governance structure.

These proposed resolutions signal a significant recalibration of Legend Holdings’ capital management tools and internal oversight framework, potentially streamlining decision-making while giving the board wide latitude to tap debt and equity markets through 2029. For shareholders, the combination of a profit distribution proposal, enhanced share repurchase authority, and large-scale debt-issuance mandate underscores a more active balance-sheet strategy that could influence returns, leverage levels, and the company’s long-term strategic positioning in the market.

More about Legend Holdings Corporation Class H

Legend Holdings Corporation is a joint stock company incorporated in the People’s Republic of China and listed in Hong Kong under stock code 03396. The group operates as an investment holding company with a diversified portfolio, and its activities are overseen by a board of directors and, currently, a board of supervisors, in line with PRC corporate governance practices.

data.title: China Aluminum Cans Calls 2026 AGM, Proposes Dividend and Share Issue Mandate
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from China Aluminum Cans Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:6898) ) is now available.

China Aluminum Cans Holdings Limited has called its annual general meeting for 22 May 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will review the audited financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025 and vote on a proposed final dividend of HK0.2 cent per share. The meeting will also consider the re-election of one executive and one independent non-executive director, confirm directors’ and auditor’s remuneration, re-appoint the auditor, and grant the board a general mandate to issue up to 20% of the company’s share capital, providing additional flexibility for future financing and capital management.

If approved, the dividend will return a modest portion of earnings to shareholders, while the re-election of directors and re-appointment of the auditor signal continuity in governance and oversight. The proposed share issuance mandate, in line with Hong Kong listing practices, enhances the company’s ability to raise capital or pursue strategic opportunities without convening separate shareholder meetings, which may influence future dilution levels and the company’s financial strategy.

More about China Aluminum Cans Holdings Ltd.

China Aluminum Cans Holdings Limited, incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong, operates in the manufacturing sector with a focus on aluminum can products. The company serves the packaging market, supplying metal containers used across various consumer and industrial applications, and is regulated under the Rules Governing the Listing of Securities on the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

The latest update is out from China Resources Pharmaceutical Group Ltd. ( (HK:3320) ).

China Resources Pharmaceutical Group Limited has announced that its non-wholly-owned subsidiary, China Resources Pharmaceutical Commercial Group Company Limited, has completed the public issuance of the second tranche of its 2026 corporate bonds in mainland China. The bonds, issued in a principal amount of RMB1.4 billion, carry a three-year term with a coupon rate of 1.65% per annum, reflecting the group’s continued access to relatively low-cost funding in the onshore bond market.

The company plans to allocate the net proceeds toward production-related expenditures, including replenishing working capital, repaying interest-bearing debt, and funding project investments in compliance with relevant regulations. This financing move is expected to support the subsidiary’s operational liquidity and expansion needs, potentially strengthening the group’s capital structure and underpinning its growth strategy in China’s pharmaceutical distribution and manufacturing sectors.

More about China Resources Pharmaceutical Group Ltd.

China Resources Pharmaceutical Group Limited is a Hong Kong-incorporated pharmaceutical company with operations in mainland China, focusing on pharmaceutical manufacturing and commercial distribution. Its non-wholly-owned subsidiary, China Resources Pharmaceutical Commercial Group Company Limited, plays a key role in the group’s financing and business activities in the PRC market.

Hangzhou Tigermed Consulting Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:3347) ) has issued an update.

Hangzhou Tigermed Consulting has released its unaudited first quarterly report for the 2026 financial year, prepared under China Accounting Standards for Business Enterprises. The document, published in both Chinese and English with the Chinese version prevailing in case of discrepancies, outlines the group’s early-year financial performance for shareholders and public investors.

The company emphasizes that the figures have not been audited, signaling that results may be subject to adjustments as the year progresses and as full reviews are completed. The disclosure, made under Hong Kong listing and securities regulations, reflects Tigermed’s ongoing compliance with transparency requirements and provides stakeholders with preliminary insight into its 2026 operating trajectory.
